claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand a bit about your situation.

**First, let me ask about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for? The eligibility rules differ depending on their age:
- Under 16 years old
- 16-19 years old
- 20 or over

Please tell me the age(s) of your child/children, and their names so I can help assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess the eligibility for your son Alex, who is 13 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

For a 13-year-old, this is typically straightforward - most children this age are not in full-time work. Please confirm if Alex is either:
- Not working at all, OR
- Working less than 24 hours per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's only 13, so he's just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that's what I expected for a 13-year-old. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

In other words, does Alex receive any benefits such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

...in his own name? 

For most 13-year-olds in school, the answer would be that they're NOT receiving these benefits. Can you confirm this is the case for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - Alex doesn't get any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular school kid.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

This is relevant because certain types of apprenticeships can affect eligibility. Since Alex is 13 and in regular school, I expect he's not in an apprenticeship, but I need to confirm:

- Is Alex NOT in an apprenticeship at all?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's just doing regular school - he's only 13!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes complete sense! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to ask about **your residency status** (the claimant):

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common), OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, seeking work, or sufficient resources), OR
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me check if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are you living abroad in any of these specific situations?**

1. **Crown servant** - Are you working abroad as a Crown servant (e.g., diplomatic service, armed forces)?

2. **Qualifying countries** - Are you living in certain countries where UK Child Benefit can still be claimed?

Or, do any of these other residency paths apply to you:

3. Do you have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme?

4. Do you have **pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme and meet certain conditions (such as working, studying, actively seeking work, or having sufficient resources)?

Could you tell me more about your situation while living abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm not a Crown servant, I don't have settled or pre-settled status, and I'm just living abroad in a regular situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, I need to inform you of the outcome based on the residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that based on your situation, **you are not eligible for UK Child Benefit**.

**Reason for ineligibility:**
You do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet special circumstances such as having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), working abroad as a Crown servant, or living in certain qualifying countries

**What this means:**
Since you're living abroad without any of the special qualifying circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex, even though he would otherwise qualify as a 13-year-old child in regular education.

**Important to note:**
- If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time
- Child Benefit eligibility is based on the claimant's residency status, not the child's

If you have any questions about your specific situation or think there might be other circumstances that could apply, I'd recommend contacting HMRC directly for personalized advice.

Let me now record the final eligibility outcome:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: The assessment is now complete. I'm sorry the outcome isn't what you were hoping for, but I hope this explanation has been helpful in understanding the requirements. If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and meets all child-related criteria: he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, eligibility for Child Benefit requires the claimant to meet UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not meet any of the special circumstances that would allow claiming from abroad (not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in a qualifying country). Therefore, the claim cannot proceed due to the claimant's residency status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who meets all child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit, as they are living abroad without any qualifying special circumstances. Child Benefit eligibility depends on both the child qualifying and the claimant meeting residency requirements.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
